recent_posts.text:
Cuy Sheffield: The Soul of Agentic Commerce
Agentic commerce will only scale when AI agents develop unique identities that let them generate their own goals, creating autonomous demand for machine-payable services.
> Give agents taste and the ability to communicate, and commerce follows on its own.
> We need to give them somewhere to be from, something to carry, and the freedom to want what people like them want, then, demand stops being a queue of our ideas and becomes a population of theirs.
> Stop writing tasks. Start writing souls.
Source:
https://x.com/cuysheffield/status/2074913360093773853

recent_posts.text:
Grok 4.5 from SpaceXAI ranks 4th on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index
Build at far lower cost, and shows major gains in agentic performance and efficiency over Grok 4.3
https://x.com/artificialanlys/status/2074956932289282087?s=46&t=2q9OLMlaobNlHLewOXT4uA

tweet.text:
SpaceXAI’s Grok 4.5 scores 54 to place fourth on the Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index following only Fable 5, GPT-5.5, and Opus 4.8. It scores on par with GPT-5.5 in Codex on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index in the Grok Build harness, at much lower cost

Grok 4.5 improves 16 points over Grok 4.3 on the Intelligence Index, bringing SpaceXAI to the intelligence frontier behind only OpenAI and Anthropic, and outperforming all open weights models and notably Google’s Gemini models. Key standout areas of performance are agentic knowledge work and coding.

Grok 4.5 in Grok Build scores 76 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, on par with GPT-5.5 (xhigh) in Codex and just below Fable 5 (max) in Claude Code, and at a small fraction of the token usage and price.

Congratulations to @SpaceXAI, @cursor_ai, and @elonmusk on the impressive release!

Key Takeaways:

➤ Grok 4.5 performs very strongly on agentic tasks. Grok 4.5 ranks #4 on GDPval-AA v2 with an Elo of 1543, bet

recent_posts.text:
SK Hynix's $28 billion US listing is multiple times oversubscribed ahead of Thursday's pricing. The South Korean memory chipmaker has drawn strong early demand from global long-only funds and tech-focused investors, putting the offering on track to become the biggest-ever US listing by a foreign company.
link

CXMT will open investor subscriptions next week for a Shanghai IPO that could raise more than $5 billion, the biggest listing in mainland China since 2022. The Hefei-based memory chipmaker, a rival to Samsung and SK Hynix, is offering 6.688 billion shares on the STAR Board and targeting at least 29.5 billion yuan ($4.3 billion), a deal that would also rank as Asia's largest since CATL's share sale last year.
link

tweet.text: Erling Haaland’s perspective on feeling tired:

Announcing our $130M Series A to build the Open Superintelligence Stack

Led by Radical Ventures, with NVIDIA, Intel Capital, Dell Capital, and existing investors

Train, deploy, and continuously improve your own models using our stack.

Own your intelligence.

This girl group in the video is KISS OF LIFE, a four-member group, including Julie, Natty, Belle, and Haneul. Natty, one of the most famous members, was on idol shows SIXTEEN (the show that formed Twice) and Idol School. Before KISS OF LIFE, she solo debuted with song “Nineteen” and continued with “Sugarcoat”, a fan favorite.

The song in the video is the first verse of “Sticky,” KISS OF LIFE’s Korean breakout song. Sticky was their first music show win and had strong charting. “Igloo” and “Midas Touch” were the songs that got the world’s attention, garnering 243M and 166M streams on Spotify. Without attending the event, we suspect those were the songs they performed, with potentially their most recent single, “Who is She.”

KISS OF LIFE overall presents a confident sensuality with mature adult members. Both qualities were rare, when Kpop industry is overflown average 15-year-old groups doing cutesy concepts.

> In this episode, we cover where value accrues in the agent economy,  stablecoins vs. cards and the value propositions, the trust problem with open x402 endpoints, autonomous agents, headless merchants, why agents buying tokens will dwarf everything else, and why he thinks agents should stay accountable to a human.
👇
Source:
https://x.com/Shoalresearch/status/2074949406592586125?s=20

tweet.text:
Where Does Value Accrue in the Agent Economy? Visa's Head of Crypto on stablecoins, x402/MPP, and card networks

Shoal Signal with @cuysheffield, Head of Crypto at @Visa, hosted by @zaddycoin

We cover where value accrues in the agent economy,  stablecoins vs. cards and the value propositions, the trust problem with open x402 endpoints, autonomous agents, headless merchants, why agents buying tokens will dwarf everything else, and why he thinks agents should stay accountable to a human.

0:00 Intro
1:49 Where does the value accrue
2:31 Payment networks vs payment infrastructure
5:24 Trust and governance is the missing piece
6:37 Anyone can spin up an x402 endpoint
7:53 Why a crypto vet won't give agents a wallet
10:17 Headless merchants are the big opportunity
11:35 Sell workflows, not tools
13:43 The demand side is the harder problem
19:25 Where the first billion gets spent
23:15 Token share on inference
25:02 Problems with agent spend
26:49 Agents buying tokens dwarfs everything
33:0

Openai Introduces GPT-Live: A New Generation of Voice Models for Natural Human-AI Interaction
> Built on a full-duplex architecture, GPT-Live can listen and speak at the same time. During conversations, it can show it’s paying attention with phrases like “mhmm” or “yeah”
> It can hand off complex tasks like web search and reasoning to GPT-5.5 in the background.
Source:
https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live/

Why Are You in Such a Rush?
> The world wants you to rush into everything. Rushed decisions. Rushed conversations
> In doing so, you give up your claim on your own life
> In a world that wants you to rush, the ultimate act of rebellion is presence.
> Be in the season you're in. Don't romanticize the past, don't fantasize the future.
> Every single thing you do today is something your younger self dreamed of.
> Something your older self will wish they could go back and do.
> The good old days are happening, right now.
> So, where are you going and why are you in such a rush?
Source:
https://x.com/SahilBloom/status/2072660676749774972

tweet.text:
WARNING: Longer post (but worth reading or bookmarking for later).

Your life has seasons.

Each one is unique. Characterized by its own distinct desires, struggles, opportunities, and identity.

But one reflection I've had recently is just how easy it is to completely disassociate with the present season.

To give all your time and energy toward a longing for some nostalgic memory of a prior season or an anticipation for some beautiful state of a future season.

You look back at the past and all you see is sunshine. Because it all worked out. You forget (or glaze over) the struggle you endured. You're here today. You made it. You're alive. You're doing fine.

You look forward at the future and dream on what could be. You'll have so much more. More freedom. More purpose. More health. More deep connection. More everything.

The past is beautiful and the future feels limitless. So, logically, you slowly start to treat everything about the present as the bridge. A dash connecting your pas

Launching @usescout_trade which is built by an ex-xAI, Anthropic, and Princeton team bringing an AI quant to retail traders via USDC.

They have 8 strategies live today, with more coming soon, including strategies inspired by MrBeast, the All In Podcast, Trump, and Druckenmiller.

Each strategy continuously analyzes YouTube, X, Reddit, and major news sources to track its theme or persona, using AI to inform portfolio updates and rebalancing over time.

Try it out and let us know what you think. Hit the team with feedback on DMs

recent_posts.text:
The Collateral Dollar: Stablecoins and the Haircut Channel in the Offshore Dollar System
“The collateral dollar exists only when the claim above the stablecoin survives the move from token liquidity to bank-dollar liquidity.”
> Stablecoins should be viewed not just as digital dollars but as collateral that can support new layers of offshore dollar credit, with the real monetary expansion occurring when financial institutions issue and fund liabilities against them.
> The growth and stability of this "collateral dollar" system depend on legal control, haircuts, funding markets, and institutional trust, not simply on the stablecoin's reserves or peg.
👇
Source:
https://x.com/borjaneira_/status/2074485951934435702

tweet.text:
We are excited to launch 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗲 𝗕𝗬𝗢𝗖 (Bring-your-own-Cloud) today.

It is fully managed @restatedev, with all the features of Restate Cloud, but in your own account, in a dedicated VPC. Data never leaves your infra. 

Restate BYOC is already live in production with multiple customers, powering big workloads for AI agents and workflow backends. It has run loads of 100k durable actions/sec. And today we’re finally publicly announcing it.

Learn more here, and let us know what you think!

https://www.restate.dev/blog/announcing-restate-byoc?utm_source=x&utm_campaign=byoc-launch&utm_content=founder
